entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
https://bitsum.com/download-process-lasso/
Launch Fortnite and sit in the lobby. FortniteClient-Win64-Shipping.exe CPU Affinity -> Always -> Select CPU Affinity. CPU Priority -> Always, and set it to High go to Efficiency Mode -> Always -> OFF.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Open Command Prompt (Admin) and run these exact commands one by one:

    bcdedit /set useplatformclock no

        Why: This forces Windows to use the TSC (Time Stamp Counter) instead of the slower HPET. This is the single most important tweak for 0.1% low stability.

    bcdedit /set disabledynamictick yes

        Why: This stops the CPU from stopping its clock during idle moments. It keeps the CPU "awake" and ready to process a frame at all times.

    bcdedit /set tscsyncpolicy enhanced

        Why: Specifically for the 14900K’s hybrid architecture, this ensures all 24 cores are perfectly synchronized in time.

    bcdedit /set useplatformtick no

        Why: Prevents Windows from using an extra synthetic layer for time-keeping, further reducing kernel-level latency.

    bcdedit /set hypervisorlaunchtype off

        Why: This is the "Nuclear Option" to ensure VBS/Core Isolation is dead. If this is ON, your CPU is running in a "virtual bubble," adding ~5-10% latency to every frame.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
Run CMD as Admin: bcdedit /set tscsyncpolicy Enhanced (You did this).

The Add-on: bcdedit /set clockres 10000

Why: This sets the global kernel timer resolution to exactly 0.5ms (10,000 units). While tools like "Timer Resolution" do this in the app, setting it at the BCD level ensures it's active from the second you boot.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
Download ParkControl (Bitsum).

Select Bitsum Highest Performance.

Set Parking and Frequency Scaling to 100% (Disabled) for all core types.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Device Manager (The Deep Clean):

    Click View -> Show hidden devices.

    Expand Universal Serial Bus controllers.

    Right-click every USB Root Hub and Generic USB Hub -> Properties -> Power Management.

    Uncheck "Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power."

    Pro Tip: Do the same for Human Interface Devices (HID). Your mouse and keyboard are often listed here as "HID-compliant device." Disable power savings for all of them.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PowerSettings\2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3\d4e23884-9b93-478a-9e23-77356611f124

Set Attributes to 2. (This makes the hidden "USB 3 Link Power Management" visible in your Power Plan settings so you can disable it).

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
Go to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PowerThrottling (If it doesn't exist, create the key).

Create a DWORD (32-bit) named PowerThrottlingOff and set it to 1.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
DISABLE ALL
High Precision Event Timer (HPET): The Most Controversial. Pros disable this to remove the "Timer" overhead, allowing the CPU to use its own raw TSC (Time Stamp Counter). This often fixes micro-stutters in Fortnite.

Microsoft System Management BIOS Driver: Purely for corporate inventory tracking.

NDIS Virtual Network Adapter Enumerator: Only needed if you use complex Virtual Machines.

UMBus Root Bus Enumerator: An old legacy device for "User-Mode" drivers.

Intel(R) Management Engine Interface: This is a "backdoor" processor inside your CPU. Disabling it can reduce DPC latency, though some people report it affects boot times. For a pro rig, we disable it.

Programmable Interrupt Controller: Legacy hardware that interferes with modern MSI-mode devices.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
Interrupt Affinity Pinning (The Tool)

Download the Interrupt Affinity Policy Tool (a classic Microsoft utility often found on TechPowerUp).

    Run IntPolicy.exe as Administrator.

    Find the USB xHCI Compliant Host Controller that matches the "Location Path" of your mouse.

    Click Set Mask.

    The Pro-Strat for 14900K:

        Core 0 & 1: Never use these. Windows bloat lives here.

        Core 2: Assign your GPU here.

        Core 4: Assign your Mouse USB Controller here.

        Core 6: Assign your Keyboard USB Controller here

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
https://github.com/Sathango/Msi-Utility-v3/blob/main/Msi%20Utility%20v3.exe
Download the "Interrupt Affinity Policy Tool" (msi-util-v3).

Run it as Admin. Find your NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti.

Check the box for MSI (Message Signaled Interrupts).

Change the Interrupt Priority to High.

Click Set Mask and select ONLY CPU 2 and CPU 3 (These are the second physical P-Core).

    Note: CPU 0 and 1 are usually busy with Windows kernel tasks. By moving the GPU to Core 2/3, you give it a "private lane" to the CPU.\

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Open NVIDIA Control Panel.

Go to Program Settings -> Add.

Browse to C:\Windows\explorer.exe.

Set Power Management Mode to Prefer Maximum Performance.

Why: Windows often drops the GPU clock slightly when it thinks you are "just on the desktop" or in a menu. Forcing Explorer to Max Performance keeps the GPU voltage rails from dipping when you transition from the lobby to the bus.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
The Tweak: In Profile Inspector, look for:

    0x20891540 (Memory Allocation Policy): Set this to 0x00000001 (Aggressive).

    0x20D833C1 (Toggle FXAA Indicator): Set to 0x00000000 (Off).

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content: https://www.dell.com/support/home/en-au/drivers/driversdetails?driverid=j9g8k

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Interrupt Moderation: Disabled. 

Flow Control: Disabled.

Gigabit Lite / Green Ethernet / Power Saving Mode: Disabled.

Jumbo Packet: Disabled.

Large Send Offload (IPv4 & IPv6): Disabled. 

Speed & Duplex: Set to your maximum (e.g., 1.0 Gbps Full Duplex or 2.5 Gbps Full Duplex). Do not leave on "Auto-Negotiation."

Receive Side Scaling (RSS): Enabled.

Go to the Power Management tab and Uncheck "Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power."

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
⬇️ 
Cloudflare DNS: Go to your Network Settings and manually set your DNS to 1.1.1.1 and 1.0.0.1. This is the fastest public DNS for gaming.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
Open Registry Editor (regedit).

Navigate to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters

Right-click in the right pane and create/modify these DWORD (32-bit) values:

    MaxFreeTcbs: Set to 65536. (Increases the number of active connections Windows can handle before it starts dropping or delaying packets).

    MaxUserPort: Set to 65534. (Opens the maximum range of ports for the game to communicate through).

    TcpTimedWaitDelay: Set to 30. (Forces Windows to close "dead" connections faster so they don't sit around and bloat your network stack).

    GlobalMaxTcpWindowSize: Set to 65535.

    Tcp1323Opts: Set to 1. (Enables "Window Scaling" which allows the PC to handle larger bursts of data without stalling).

    DefaultTTL: Set to 64. (The standard "hop" limit for gaming packets).

registry_paths:
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters

Right-click in the right pane and create

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
Press Win + R, type gpedit.msc, and hit Enter.

Go to Computer Configuration -> Windows Settings -> Policy-based QoS.

Right-click it and select Create new policy.

    Name: Fortnite_Priority

    DSCP Value: 46 (This is the "Expedited Forwarding" tag used by pros).

    Click Next.

Select Only for executable name and type: FortniteClient-Win64-Shipping.exe.

Keep clicking Next/Finish until the policy is created.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Go to waveform.com/tools/bufferbloat and run the test.

The Goal: If you get anything less than an A+, your router is the bottleneck.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Open PowerShell (Admin).

Paste this: powercfg -duplicatescheme e9a42b02-d5df-448d-aa00-03f14749eb61

This unlocks the "Ultimate Performance" power plan. Go to your Control Panel Power Options and select it.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content: nside that plan, ensure Link State Power Management for PCI Express is OFF. This prevents your 4070 Ti from ever entering a low-power state.\

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
tFAW (Four Activate Window): Set to 16. (This is the lowest stable value for almost all DDR5).

tRRDS (Row to Row Delay Short): Set to 4.

tRRDL (Row to Row Delay Long): Set to 8.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Press Win + R, type regedit, and hit Enter.

Navigate to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces

You will see several folders with long IDs. Click through them until you find the one that contains your IP address (look for the key DhcpIPAddress).

Inside that folder, right-click and create two new DWORD (32-bit) Values:

    Name one: TcpAckFrequency -> Set value to 1.

    Name the other: TCPNoDelay -> Set value to 1.

This forces Windows to send every packet the instant it is generated.

registry_paths:
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces

You will see several folders with long IDs

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
⬇️ 
In Registry Editor, go to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile

Find NetworkThrottlingIndex.

Double-click it and change the value to ffffffff (Hexadecimal). This completely disables the throttle.

In the same folder, find SystemResponsiveness. Set it to 0.

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel

Right-click -> New -> DWORD (32-bit) Value.

Name it: GlobalTimerResolutionRequests

Set the value to 1.

    Why: This forces Windows to respect the 0.5ms timer resolution

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management

Create/Modify these DWORD (32-bit) values:

    FeatureSettingsOverride -> Set to 3

    FeatureSettingsOverrideMask -> Set to 3

Navigate to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel

    DisableExceptionChainValidation -> Set to 1

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\PriorityControl

Value: Win32PrioritySeparation

The Pro Value: Set this to 26 (Hexadecimal).

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
🆕 
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games

Values to change:

    GPU Priority: Set to 8.

    Priority: Set to 6.

    Scheduling Category: Set to High.

    SFIO Priority: Set to High.

Path: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile

    SystemResponsiveness: Set to 0 (This disables the 20% "safety" reserve Windows keeps for background apps).

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
(Testing)
Download - **aida64** memory benchmark note latency (ns) aim to lower this.
**Y-cruncher** run the vst to test ddr5 ram
**Hwinfo64** make sure under 50c
**Capframex** benchmarks latency 
**basic**
• Fresh Reinstall windows to avoid bloat using a usb (Windows X-Lite 'Micro 11 24H2)
•Update all drivers 
•Research NVIDIA CLEAN INSTALL and Display Driver Uninstaller 
•Update bios 

(Advanced)
• Download scewin from GitHub 
•extract zip run export.bat open the file that dumps using notepad++, 
Disable **spread spectrum(or clock spread)**,
Disable **socket power saving** or **pcie power saving** or **usb phy power down**
Enable EHCI
Enable Pci delay optimization save the .txt run import .bat 
•Tighten ram timings using thaiphoon
•Enable Resizeable bar and make sure it’s on using nvidia profile inspector

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Ram timings (Too Be Continued)
try 32-38-38-76 or 30-36-36-72

Cr 2n
TCL 
TRCD 
TRCDW 
Changing any of these won’t really do much if only changed by one or two 
Trp 
Tras
up dram voltage to 1.4 or 1.45
tRFC: 450-550 (lower for latency, but test—too low causes errors). 
tRRDS/tRRDL: 4-6/6-8.
tFAW: 16-24.
tREFI: Increase to 65000+ (delays refreshes for performance, but monitor heat)
Stability/Heat Check: HWInfo for temps (<60°C RAM ideal)

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Cpu
Run cinebench r23 pay attention to vcore voltage on hwinfo64 
Aim for 37000 pts 
Aim for 1.2 vcore 
Go in bios set cpu loadline calibration to mode 3 and lite load mode 3 
adaptive + offset - 0.070 -0.130 or 0.100 for some cpus

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Open Command Prompt (Admin) and run:

    fsutil behavior set disablelastaccess 1 (Prevents Windows from writing a "timestamp" every time a game file is read—saves SSD cycles).

    fsutil behavior set disable8dot3 1 (Disables legacy MS-DOS filename creation).

    fsutil behavior set encryptpagingfile 0 (Ensures your page file isn't being encrypted on the fly).

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content: https://en.softonic.com/download/usb-device-tree-viewer/windows/post-download?dt=internalDownload

entries.author: diggyt
entries.content:
Open UsbTreeView.

It will show a beautiful tree of your ports.

Move your mouse. The port it’s plugged into will usually turn green or have a mouse icon.

Click that port. In the right-hand window, it will tell you exactly which Host Controller it belongs to.

Note the Device ID or Service Name
